title: astroloma xerophyllum
intro: Astroloma xerophyllum, also known as the Western Club-Reseda, is a type of flowering plant native to Western Australia. It is a small, shrub-like plant with white flowers and is used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties. The plant is said to have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which may help to soothe skin irritations and wounds, as well as exhibit antioxidant and anti-cancer properties. In herbal preparations, Astroloma xerophyllum is often made into a tea, tincture, or salve to be used topically or internally for its health benefits.
